You are working on the ankidroid/Anki-Android repository.
The codebase is at version 2.171 (commit: 7a65160e).

## Issue to Resolve

Regression: can't select deck in add screen
Also, I can't change decks in the note editor


https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/assets/69634269/91e7b6db-ef3e-4048-b9dd-30545b4405ce

_Originally posted by @BrayanDSO in https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/issues/14171#issuecomment-1697548993_

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/DeckSpinnerSelection.kt`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

## Important Evaluation Notes

**Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing.
**If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files

## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS

**NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

1→/*

  7→ * version.
  8→ *
  9→ * This program is distributed in the hope that it will be useful, but WITHOUT ANY
 10→ * WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty of MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A
 11→ * PARTICULAR PURPOSE. See the GNU General Public License for more details.
 12→ *
 13→ * You should have received a copy of the GNU General Public License along with
 14→ * this program.  If not, see <http://www.gnu.org/licenses/>.
 15→ */
 16→package com.ichi2.anki
 17→
 18→import android.view.MotionEvent
 19→import android.view.View
 20→import android.view.ViewGroup
 21→import android.widget.ArrayAdapter
 22→import android.widget.Spinner
 23→import android.widget.TextView
 24→import androidx.appcompat.app.ActionBar
 25→import com.ichi2.anki.dialogs.DeckSelectionDialog
 26→import com.ichi2.anki.dialogs.DeckSelectionDialog.SelectableDeck
 27→import com.ichi2.anki.dialogs.DeckSelectionDialog.SelectableDeck.Companion.fromCollection
 28→import com.ichi2.anki.widgets.DeckDropDownAdapter
 29→import com.ichi2.libanki.*
 30→import com.ichi2.libanki.Collection
 31→import com.ichi2.utils.FragmentManagerSupplier
 32→import com.ichi2.utils.asFragmentManagerSupplier
 33→import timber.log.Timber
 34→
 35→/**
 36→ * Handles expansion of a [Spinner], allowing a press to display a [DeckSelectionDialog]
 37→ * Responsible for filtering the decks to display in the [DeckSelectionDialog]
 38→ *
 39→ * Populates the spinner with data, and handles display of the selected deck within the spinner control
 40→ *
 41→ * @param spinner
 42→ * The spinner displayed in the activity.
 43→ * Empty at construction. After initialization, it contains in this order:
 44→ * 1. "All decks" if [showAllDecks] is true
 45→ * 2. All decks from [mAllDeckIds].
 46→ * @param showAllDecks Whether the deck selection should allow "All Decks" as an option
 47→ * @param alwaysShowDefault If true, never hide the default deck. If false, match [DeckPicker]'s logic
 48→ * @param showFilteredDecks whether to show filtered decks
 49→ */
 50→class DeckSpinnerSelection(
 51→    private val context: AnkiActivity,
 52→    private val collection: Collection,
 53→    private val spinner: Spinner,
 54→    private val showAllDecks: Boolean,
 55→    private val alwaysShowDefault: Boolean,
 56→    private val showFilteredDecks: Boolean
 57→) {
 58→    /**
 59→     * All of the decks shown to the user.
 60→     */
 61→    private lateinit var mAllDeckIds: ArrayList<Long>
 62→
 63→    private val mFragmentManagerSupplier: FragmentManagerSupplier = context.asFragmentManagerSupplier()
 64→
 65→    lateinit var dropDownDecks: List<DeckNameId>
 66→        private set
 67→    private var mDeckDropDownAdapter: DeckDropDownAdapter? = null
 68→
 69→    fun initializeActionBarDeckSpinner(actionBar: ActionBar) {
 70→        actionBar.setDisplayShowTitleEnabled(false)
 71→
 72→        // Add drop-down menu to select deck to action bar.
 73→        dropDownDecks = computeDropDownDecks(includeFiltered = showFilteredDecks)
 74→        mAllDeckIds = ArrayList(dropDownDecks.size)
 75→        for (d in dropDownDecks) {
 76→            val thisDid = d.id
 77→            mAllDeckIds.add(thisDid)
 78→        }
 79→        mDeckDropDownAdapter = DeckDropDownAdapter(context, dropDownDecks)
 80→        spinner.adapter = mDeckDropDownAdapter
 81→        setSpinnerListener()
 82→    }
 83→
 84→    fun initializeNoteEditorDeckSpinner() {
 85→        dropDownDecks = computeDropDownDecks(includeFiltered = false)
 86→        val deckNames = ArrayList<String>(dropDownDecks.size)
 87→        mAllDeckIds = ArrayList(dropDownDecks.size)
 88→        for (d in dropDownDecks) {
 89→            val currentName = d.name
 90→            deckNames.add(currentName)
 91→        }
 92→        val noteDeckAdapter: ArrayAdapter<String?> = object : ArrayAdapter<String?>(context, R.layout.multiline_spinner_item, deckNames as List<String?>) {
 93→            override fun getDropDownView(position: Int, convertView: View?, parent: ViewGroup): View {
 94→                // Cast the drop down items (popup items) as text view
 95→                val tv = super.getDropDownView(position, convertView, parent) as TextView
 96→
 97→                // If this item is selected
 98→                if (position == spinner.selectedItemPosition) {
 99→                    tv.setBackgroundColor(context.getColor(R.color.note_editor_selected_item_background))
100→                    tv.setTextColor(context.getColor(R.color.note_editor_selected_item_text))
101→                }
102→
103→                // Return the modified view
104→                return tv
105→            }
106→        }
107→        spinner.adapter = noteDeckAdapter
108→        setSpinnerListener()
109→    }
110→
111→    /**
112→     * @return All decks, except maybe default if it should be hidden.
113→     */
114→    fun computeDropDownDecks(includeFiltered: Boolean): List<DeckNameId> {
115→        return collection.decks.allNamesAndIds(skipEmptyDefault = true, includeFiltered = includeFiltered)
116→    }
117→
118→    fun setSpinnerListener() {
119→        spinner.setOnTouchListener { _: View?, motionEvent: MotionEvent ->
120→            if (motionEvent.action == MotionEvent.ACTION_UP) {
121→                displayDeckSelectionDialog(collection)
122→            }
123→            true
124→        }
125→        setSpinnerVisibility(View.VISIBLE)
126→    }
127→
128→    /**
129→     * Move the selected deck in the spinner to [deckId].
130→     * Timber if [deckId] is not an id of a known deck.
131→     * @param deckId The ID of the deck to select
132→     */
133→    fun updateDeckPosition(deckId: DeckId) {
134→        val position = mAllDeckIds.indexOf(deckId)
135→        if (position != -1) {
136→            spinner.setSelection(position)
137→        } else {
138→            Timber.w("updateDeckPosition() error :: deckId=%d, position=%d", deckId, position)
139→        }
140→    }
141→
142→    fun notifyDataSetChanged() {
143→        mDeckDropDownAdapter!!.notifyDataSetChanged()
144→    }
145→
146→    fun setEnabledActionBarSpinner(enabled: Boolean) {
147→        spinner.isEnabled = enabled
148→    }
149→
150→    fun setSpinnerVisibility(view: Int) {
151→        spinner.visibility = view
152→    }
153→
154→    /**
155→     * Iterates the drop down decks, and selects the one matching the given id.
156→     * @param deckId The deck id to be selected.
157→     * @param setAsCurrentDeck If true, deckId will be set as the current deck id of Collection
158→     * (this means the deck selected here will continue to appear in any future Activity whose
159→     * display data is loaded from Collection's current deck). If false, deckId will not be set as
160→     * the current deck id of Collection.
161→     * @return True if selection succeeded.
162→     */
163→    fun selectDeckById(deckId: DeckId, setAsCurrentDeck: Boolean): Boolean {
164→        return if (deckId == ALL_DECKS_ID) {
165→            selectAllDecks()
166→        } else {
167→            selectDeck(deckId, setAsCurrentDeck)
168→        }
169→    }
170→
171→    /**
172→     * select in the spinner deck with id
173→     * @param deckId The deck id to search (not ALL_DECKS_ID)
174→     * @param setAsCurrentDeck whether this deck should be selected in the collection (if it exists)
175→     * @return whether it was found
176→     */
177→    private fun selectDeck(deckId: DeckId, setAsCurrentDeck: Boolean): Boolean {
178→        for (dropDownDeckIdx in mAllDeckIds.indices) {
179→            if (mAllDeckIds[dropDownDeckIdx] == deckId) {
180→                val position = if (showAllDecks) dropDownDeckIdx + 1 else dropDownDeckIdx
181→                spinner.setSelection(position)
182→                if (setAsCurrentDeck) {
183→                    collection.decks.select(deckId)
184→                }
185→                return true
186→            }
187→        }
188→        return false
189→    }
190→
191→    /**
192→     * Select all decks. Must be called only if [showAllDecks].
193→     * @return whether selection was a success.
194→     */
195→    fun selectAllDecks(): Boolean {
196→        if (!showAllDecks) {
197→            CrashReportService.sendExceptionReport("selectAllDecks was called while `showAllDecks is false`", "DeckSpinnerSelection:selectAllDecks")
198→            return false
199→        }
200→        spinner.setSelection(0)
201→        return true
202→    }
203→
204→    /**
205→     * Displays a [DeckSelectionDialog]
206→     */
207→    fun displayDeckSelectionDialog(col: Collection?) {
208→        val decks = fromCollection(col!!, includeFiltered = false).toMutableList()
209→        if (showAllDecks) {
210→            decks.add(SelectableDeck(ALL_DECKS_ID, context.resources.getString(R.string.card_browser_all_decks)))
211→        }
212→        val dialog = DeckSelectionDialog.newInstance(context.getString(R.string.search_deck), null, false, decks)
213→        AnkiActivity.showDialogFragment(mFragmentManagerSupplier.getFragmentManager(), dialog)
214→    }
215→
216→    companion object {
217→        const val ALL_DECKS_ID = 0L
218→    }
219→}

 17→
 18→import android.view.MotionEvent

 21→import android.widget.ArrayAdapter
 22→import android.widget.Spinner
 23→import android.widget.TextView

 25→import com.ichi2.anki.dialogs.DeckSelectionDialog
 26→import com.ichi2.anki.dialogs.DeckSelectionDialog.SelectableDeck
 27→import com.ichi2.anki.dialogs.DeckSelectionDialog.SelectableDeck.Companion.fromCollection
 28→import com.ichi2.anki.widgets.DeckDropDownAdapter
 29→import com.ichi2.libanki.*
 30→import com.ichi2.libanki.Collection
 31→import com.ichi2.utils.FragmentManagerSupplier
 32→import com.ichi2.utils.asFragmentManagerSupplier
 33→import timber.log.Timber
 34→
 35→/**
 36→ * Handles expansion of a [Spinner], allowing a press to display a [DeckSelectionDialog]
 37→ * Responsible for filtering the decks to display in the [DeckSelectionDialog]
 38→ *
 39→ * Populates the spinner with data, and handles display of the selected deck within the spinner control
 40→ *
 41→ * @param spinner
 42→ * The spinner displayed in the activity.
 43→ * Empty at construction. After initialization, it contains in this order:
 44→ * 1. "All decks" if [showAllDecks] is true
 45→ * 2. All decks from [mAllDeckIds].
 46→ * @param showAllDecks Whether the deck selection should allow "All Decks" as an option
 47→ * @param alwaysShowDefault If true, never hide the default deck. If false, match [DeckPicker]'s logic
 48→ * @param showFilteredDecks whether to show filtered decks
 49→ */
 50→class DeckSpinnerSelection(
 51→    private val context: AnkiActivity,
 52→    private val collection: Collection,
 53→    private val spinner: Spinner,
 54→    private val showAllDecks: Boolean,
 55→    private val alwaysShowDefault: Boolean,
 56→    private val showFilteredDecks: Boolean
 57→) {
 58→    /**
 59→     * All of the decks shown to the user.
 60→     */
 61→    private lateinit var mAllDeckIds: ArrayList<Long>
 62→
 63→    private val mFragmentManagerSupplier: FragmentManagerSupplier = context.asFragmentManagerSupplier()
 64→
 65→    lateinit var dropDownDecks: List<DeckNameId>
 66→        private set
 67→    private var mDeckDropDownAdapter: DeckDropDownAdapter? = null
 68→
 69→    fun initializeActionBarDeckSpinner(actionBar: ActionBar) {
 70→        actionBar.setDisplayShowTitleEnabled(false)
 71→
 72→        // Add drop-down menu to select deck to action bar.
 73→        dropDownDecks = computeDropDownDecks(includeFiltered = showFilteredDecks)
 74→        mAllDeckIds = ArrayList(dropDownDecks.size)
 75→        for (d in dropDownDecks) {
 76→            val thisDid = d.id
 77→            mAllDeckIds.add(thisDid)
 78→        }
 79→        mDeckDropDownAdapter = DeckDropDownAdapter(context, dropDownDecks)
 80→        spinner.adapter = mDeckDropDownAdapter
 81→        setSpinnerListener()
 82→    }
 83→
 84→    fun initializeNoteEditorDeckSpinner() {
 85→        dropDownDecks = computeDropDownDecks(includeFiltered = false)
 86→        val deckNames = ArrayList<String>(dropDownDecks.size)
 87→        mAllDeckIds = ArrayList(dropDownDecks.size)
 88→        for (d in dropDownDecks) {
 89→            val currentName = d.name
 90→            deckNames.add(currentName)
 91→        }
 92→        val noteDeckAdapter: ArrayAdapter<String?> = object : ArrayAdapter<String?>(context, R.layout.multiline_spinner_item, deckNames as List<String?>) {
 93→            override fun getDropDownView(position: Int, convertView: View?, parent: ViewGroup): View {
 94→                // Cast the drop down items (popup items) as text view
 95→                val tv = super.getDropDownView(position, convertView, parent) as TextView
 96→
 97→                // If this item is selected
 98→                if (position == spinner.selectedItemPosition) {
 99→                    tv.setBackgroundColor(context.getColor(R.color.note_editor_selected_item_background))
100→                    tv.setTextColor(context.getColor(R.color.note_editor_selected_item_text))
101→                }
102→
103→                // Return the modified view
104→                return tv
105→            }
106→        }
107→        spinner.adapter = noteDeckAdapter
108→        setSpinnerListener()
109→    }
110→
111→    /**
112→     * @return All decks, except maybe default if it should be hidden.
113→     */
114→    fun computeDropDownDecks(includeFiltered: Boolean): List<DeckNameId> {
115→        return collection.decks.allNamesAndIds(skipEmptyDefault = true, includeFiltered = includeFiltered)
116→    }
117→
118→    fun setSpinnerListener() {
119→        spinner.setOnTouchListener { _: View?, motionEvent: MotionEvent ->
120→            if (motionEvent.action == MotionEvent.ACTION_UP) {
121→                displayDeckSelectionDialog(collection)
122→            }
123→            true
124→        }
125→        setSpinnerVisibility(View.VISIBLE)
126→    }
127→
128→    /**
129→     * Move the selected deck in the spinner to [deckId].
130→     * Timber if [deckId] is not an id of a known deck.
131→     * @param deckId The ID of the deck to select
132→     */
133→    fun updateDeckPosition(deckId: DeckId) {
134→        val position = mAllDeckIds.indexOf(deckId)
135→        if (position != -1) {
136→            spinner.setSelection(position)
137→        } else {
138→            Timber.w("updateDeckPosition() error :: deckId=%d, position=%d", deckId, position)
139→        }
140→    }
141→
142→    fun notifyDataSetChanged() {
143→        mDeckDropDownAdapter!!.notifyDataSetChanged()
144→    }
145→
146→    fun setEnabledActionBarSpinner(enabled: Boolean) {
147→        spinner.isEnabled = enabled
148→    }
149→
150→    fun setSpinnerVisibility(view: Int) {
151→        spinner.visibility = view
152→    }
153→
154→    /**
155→     * Iterates the drop down decks, and selects the one matching the given id.
156→     * @param deckId The deck id to be selected.
157→     * @param setAsCurrentDeck If true, deckId will be set as the current deck id of Collection
158→     * (this means the deck selected here will continue to appear in any future Activity whose
159→     * display data is loaded from Collection's current deck). If false, deckId will not be set as
160→     * the current deck id of Collection.
161→     * @return True if selection succeeded.
162→     */
163→    fun selectDeckById(deckId: DeckId, setAsCurrentDeck: Boolean): Boolean {
164→        return if (deckId == ALL_DECKS_ID) {
165→            selectAllDecks()
166→        } else {
167→            selectDeck(deckId, setAsCurrentDeck)
168→        }
169→    }
170→
171→    /**
172→     * select in the spinner deck with id
173→     * @param deckId The deck id to search (not ALL_DECKS_ID)
174→     * @param setAsCurrentDeck whether this deck should be selected in the collection (if it exists)
175→     * @return whether it was found
176→     */
177→    private fun selectDeck(deckId: DeckId, setAsCurrentDeck: Boolean): Boolean {
178→        for (dropDownDeckIdx in mAllDeckIds.indices) {
179→            if (mAllDeckIds[dropDownDeckIdx] == deckId) {
180→                val position = if (showAllDecks) dropDownDeckIdx + 1 else dropDownDeckIdx
181→                spinner.setSelection(position)
182→                if (setAsCurrentDeck) {
183→                    collection.decks.select(deckId)
184→                }
185→                return true
186→            }
187→        }
188→        return false
189→    }
190→
191→    /**
192→     * Select all decks. Must be called only if [showAllDecks].
193→     * @return whether selection was a success.
194→     */
195→    fun selectAllDecks(): Boolean {
196→        if (!showAllDecks) {
197→            CrashReportService.sendExceptionReport("selectAllDecks was called while `showAllDecks is false`", "DeckSpinnerSelection:selectAllDecks")
198→            return false
199→        }
200→        spinner.setSelection(0)
201→        return true
202→    }
203→
204→    /**
205→     * Displays a [DeckSelectionDialog]
206→     */
207→    fun displayDeckSelectionDialog(col: Collection?) {
208→        val decks = fromCollection(col!!, includeFiltered = false).toMutableList()
209→        if (showAllDecks) {
210→            decks.add(SelectableDeck(ALL_DECKS_ID, context.resources.getString(R.string.card_browser_all_decks)))
211→        }
212→        val dialog = DeckSelectionDialog.newInstance(context.getString(R.string.search_deck), null, false, decks)
213→        AnkiActivity.showDialogFragment(mFragmentManagerSupplier.getFragmentManager(), dialog)
214→    }
215→
216→    companion object {
217→        const val ALL_DECKS_ID = 0L
218→    }
219→}
220→

val editor = getNoteEditorAddingNote(DECK_LIST, NoteEditor::class.java) 307→ editor.setCurrentlySelectedModel(col.notetypes.byName("Basic")!!.getLong("id")) 308→ val field = editor.getFieldForTest(0) 309→ field.clipboard!!.setPrimaryClip(ClipData.newHtmlText("text", "text", """text""")) 310→ assertTrue(field.clipboard!!.hasPrimaryClip()) 311→ assertNotNull(field.clipboard!!.primaryClip) 312→ 313→ // test pasting in the middle (cursor mode: selecting) 314→ editor.setField(0, "012345") 315→ field.setSelection(1, 2) // selecting "1" 316→ assertTrue(field.pastePlainText()) 317→ assertEquals("0text2345", field.fieldText) 318→ assertEquals(5, field.selectionStart) 319→ assertEquals(5, field.selectionEnd) 320→ 321→ // test pasting in the middle (cursor mode: selecting backwards) 322→ editor.setField(0, "012345") 323→ field.setSelection(2, 1) // selecting "1" 324→ assertTrue(field.pastePlainText()) 325→ assertEquals("0text2345", field.fieldText) 326→ assertEquals(5, field.selectionStart) 327→ assertEquals(5, field.selectionEnd) 328→ 329→ // test pasting in the middle (cursor mode: normal) 330→ editor.setField(0, "012345") 331→ field.setSelection(4) // after "3" 332→ assertTrue(field.pastePlainText()) 333→ assertEquals("0123text45", field.fieldText) 334→ assertEquals(8, field.selectionStart) 335→ assertEquals(8, field.selectionEnd) 336→ 337→ // test pasting at the start 338→ editor.setField(0, "012345") 339→ field.setSelection(0) // before "0" 340→ assertTrue(field.pastePlainText()) 341→ assertEquals("text012345", field.fieldText) 342→ assertEquals(4, field.selectionStart) 343→ assertEquals(4, field.selectionEnd) 344→ 345→ // test pasting at the end 346→ editor.setField(0, "012345") 347→ field.setSelection(6) // after "5" 348→ assertTrue(field.pastePlainText()) 349→ assertEquals("012345text", field.fieldText) 350→ assertEquals(10, field.selectionStart) 351→ assertEquals(10, field.selectionEnd) 352→ } 353→ 354→ private fun getCopyNoteIntent(editor: NoteEditor): Intent { 355→ val editorShadow = shadowOf(editor) 356→ editor.copyNote() 357→ return editorShadow.peekNextStartedActivityForResult().intent 358→ } 359→ 360→ private val cardCount: Int 361→ get() = col.cardCount() 362→ 363→ private fun getNoteEditorAdding(noteType: NoteType): NoteEditorTestBuilder { 364→ val n = makeNoteForType(noteType) 365→ return NoteEditorTestBuilder(n) 366→ } 367→ 368→ private fun makeNoteForType(noteType: NoteType): NotetypeJson? { 369→ return when (noteType) { 370→ NoteType.BASIC -> col.notetypes.byName("Basic") 371→ NoteType.CLOZE -> col.notetypes.byName("Cloze") 372→ NoteType.BACK_TO_FRONT -> { 373→ val name = super.addNonClozeModel("Reversed", arrayOf("Front", "Back"), "{{Back}}", "{{Front}}") 374→ col.notetypes.byName(name) 375→ } 376→ NoteType.THREE_FIELD_INVALID_TEMPLATE -> { 377→ val name = super.addNonClozeModel("Invalid", arrayOf("Front", "Back", "Side"), "", "") 378→ col.notetypes.byName(name) 379→ } 380→ } 381→ } 382→ 383→ private fun openAdvancedTextEditor(n: NoteEditor, fieldIndex: Int) { 384→ n.startAdvancedTextEditor(fieldIndex) 385→ } 386→ 387→ private fun enterTextIntoField(n: NoteEditor, i: Int, newText: String) { 388→ n.setFieldValueFromUi(i, newText) 389→ } 390→ 391→ private fun getNoteEditorAddingNote(from: FromScreen, clazz: Class): T { 392→ ensureCollectionLoadIsSynchronous() 393→ val i = Intent() 394→ when (from) { 395→ REVIEWER -> i.putExtra(NoteEditor.EXTRA_CALLER, NoteEditor.CALLER_REVIEWER_ADD) 396→ DECK_LIST -> i.putExtra(NoteEditor.EXTRA_CALLER, NoteEditor.CALLER_DECKPICKER) 397→ } 398→ return super.startActivityNormallyOpenCollectionWithIntent(clazz, i) 399→ } 400→ 401→ private fun getNoteEditorEditingExistingBasicNote(front: String, back: String, from: FromScreen): NoteEditor { 402→ val n = super.addNoteUsingBasicModel(front, back) 403→ return getNoteEditorEditingExistingBasicNote(n, from, NoteEditor::class.java) 404→ } 405→ 406→ private fun getNoteEditorEditingExistingBasicNote(n: Note, from: FromScreen, clazz: Class): T { 407→ val i = Intent() 408→ when (from) { 409→ REVIEWER -> { 410→ i.putExtra(NoteEditor.EXTRA_CALLER, NoteEditor.CALLER_REVIEWER_EDIT) 411→ editorCard = n.firstCard() 412→ } 413→ DECK_LIST -> i.putExtra(NoteEditor.EXTRA_CALLER, NoteEditor.CALLER_DECKPICKER) 414→ } 415→ return super.startActivityNormallyOpenCollectionWithIntent(clazz, i) 416→ } 417→ 418→ private enum class FromScreen { 419→ DECK_LIST, REVIEWER 420→ } 421→ 422→ /** We don't use constants here to allow for additional note types to be defined */ 423→ private enum class NoteType { 424→ BASIC, CLOZE, 425→ 426→ /**Basic, but Back is on the front */ 427→ BACK_TO_FRONT, THREE_FIELD_INVALID_TEMPLATE 428→ } 429→ 430→ inner class NoteEditorTestBuilder(notetype: NotetypeJson?) { 431→ private val mNotetype: NotetypeJson 432→ private var mFirstField: String? = null 433→ private var mSecondField: String? = null 434→ private var mThirdField: String? = null 435→ fun build(): NoteEditor { 436→ val editor = build(NoteEditor::class.java) 437→ advanceRobolectricLooper() 438→ advanceRobolectricLooper() 439→ advanceRobolectricLooper() 440→ advanceRobolectricLooper() 441→ // 4 is insufficient 442→ advanceRobolectricLooper() 443→ advanceRobolectricLooper() 444→ return editor 445→ } 446→ 447→ fun build(clazz: Class): T { 448→ col.notetypes.setCurrent(mNotetype) 449→ val noteEditor = getNoteEditorAddingNote(REVIEWER, clazz) 450→ advanceRobolectricLooper() 451→ noteEditor!!.setFieldValueFromUi(0, mFirstField) 452→ if (mSecondField != null) { 453→ noteEditor.setFieldValueFromUi(1, mSecondField) 454→ } 455→ if (mThirdField != null) { 456→ noteEditor.setFieldValueFromUi(2, mThirdField) 457→ } 458→ return noteEditor 459→ } 460→ 461→ fun withNoFirstField(): NoteEditorTestBuilder { 462→ return this 463→ } 464→ 465→ fun withFirstField(text: String?): NoteEditorTestBuilder { 466→ mFirstField = text 467→ return this 468→ } 469→ 470→ fun withSecondField(text: String?): NoteEditorTestBuilder { 471→ mSecondField = text 472→ return this 473→ } 474→ 475→ fun withThirdField(text: String?): NoteEditorTestBuilder { 476→ mThirdField = text 477→ return this 478→ } 479→ 480→ init { 481→ assertNotNull(notetype) { "model was null" } 482→ mNotetype = notetype 483→ } 484→ } 485→} 486→

 70→open class RobolectricTest : AndroidTest {
 71→
 72→    @Suppress("PLATFORM_CLASS_MAPPED_TO_KOTLIN")
 73→    private fun Any.wait(timeMs: Long) = (this as Object).wait(timeMs)
 74→
 75→    private val mControllersForCleanup = ArrayList<ActivityController<*>>()
 76→
 77→    protected fun saveControllerForCleanup(controller: ActivityController<*>) {
 78→        mControllersForCleanup.add(controller)
 79→    }
 80→
 81→    protected open fun useInMemoryDatabase(): Boolean {
 82→        return true
 83→    }
 84→
 85→    @get:Rule
 86→    val mTaskScheduler = TaskSchedulerRule()
 87→
 88→    /** Allows [com.ichi2.testutils.Flaky] to annotate tests in subclasses */
 89→    @get:Rule
 90→    val ignoreFlakyTests = IgnoreFlakyTestsInCIRule()
 91→
 92→    @Before
 93→    @CallSuper
 94→    open fun setUp() {
 95→        TimeManager.resetWith(MockTime(2020, 7, 7, 7, 0, 0, 0, 10))
 96→
 97→        ChangeManager.clearSubscribers()
 98→
 99→        // resolved issues with the collection being reused if useInMemoryDatabase is false
100→        CollectionHelper.instance.setColForTests(null)
101→
102→        maybeSetupBackend()
103→
104→        // If you want to see the Android logging (from Timber), you need to set it up here
105→        ShadowLog.stream = System.out
106→
107→        Storage.setUseInMemory(useInMemoryDatabase())
108→
109→        // Reset static variable for custom tabs failure.
110→        CustomTabActivityHelper.resetFailed()
111→
112→        // See: #6140 - This global ideally shouldn't exist, but it will cause crashes if set.
113→        DialogHandler.discardMessage()
114→
115→        // BUG: We do not reset the MetaDB
116→        MetaDB.closeDB()
117→    }
118→
119→    protected open fun useLegacyHelper(): Boolean {
120→        return false
121→    }
122→
123→    protected fun getHelperFactory(): SupportSQLiteOpenHelper.Factory =
124→        if (useInMemoryDatabase()) {
125→            Timber.w("Using in-memory database for test. Collection should not be re-opened")
126→            InMemorySQLiteOpenHelperFactory()
127→        } else {
128→            FrameworkSQLiteOpenHelperFactory()
129→        }
130→
131→    @After
132→    @CallSuper
133→    open fun tearDown() {
134→        // If you don't clean up your ActivityControllers you will get OOM errors
135→        for (controller in mControllersForCleanup) {
136→            Timber.d("Calling destroy on controller %s", controller.get().toString())
137→            try {
138→                controller.destroy()
139→            } catch (e: Exception) {
140→                // Any exception here is likely because the test code already destroyed it, which is fine
141→                // No exception here should halt test execution since tests are over anyway.
142→            }
143→        }
144→        mControllersForCleanup.clear()
145→
146→        try {
147→            if (CollectionHelper.instance.colIsOpenUnsafe()) {
148→                CollectionHelper.instance.getColUnsafe(targetContext)!!.debugEnsureNoOpenPointers()
149→            }
150→            // If you don't tear down the database you'll get unexpected IllegalStateExceptions related to connections
151→            CollectionHelper.instance.closeCollection("RobolectricTest: End")
152→        } catch (ex: BackendException) {
153→            if ("CollectionNotOpen" == ex.message) {
154→                Timber.w(ex, "Collection was already disposed - may have been a problem")
155→            } else {
156→                throw ex
157→            }
158→        } finally {
159→            // After every test make sure the CollectionHelper is no longer overridden (done for null testing)
160→            disableNullCollection()
161→
162→            // called on each AnkiDroidApp.onCreate(), and spams the build
163→            // there is no onDestroy(), so call it here.
164→            Timber.uprootAll()
165→
166→            TimeManager.reset()
167→        }
168→        Dispatchers.resetMain()
169→        runBlocking { CollectionManager.discardBackend() }
170→    }
171→
172→    protected fun clickMaterialDialogButton(button: WhichButton, @Suppress("SameParameterValue") checkDismissed: Boolean) {
173→        val dialog = ShadowDialog.getLatestDialog() as MaterialDialog
174→        dialog.getActionButton(button).performClick()
175→        if (checkDismissed) {
176→            Assert.assertTrue("Dialog not dismissed?", Shadows.shadowOf(dialog).hasBeenDismissed())
177→        }
178→    }
179→
180→    /**
181→     * Click on a dialog button for an AlertDialog dialog box. Replaces the above helper.
182→     */
183→    protected fun clickAlertDialogButton(button: Int, @Suppress("SameParameterValue") checkDismissed: Boolean) {
184→        val dialog = ShadowDialog.getLatestDialog() as AlertDialog
185→
186→        dialog.getButton(button).performClick()
187→        // Need to run UI thread tasks to actually run the onClickHandler
188→        ShadowLooper.runUiThreadTasks()
189→
190→        if (checkDismissed) {
191→            Assert.assertTrue("Dialog not dismissed?", Shadows.shadowOf(dialog).hasBeenDismissed())
192→        }
193→    }
194→
195→    /**
196→     * Get the current dialog text. Will return null if no dialog visible *or* if you check for dismissed and it has been dismissed
197→     *
198→     * @param checkDismissed true if you want to check for dismissed, will return null even if dialog exists but has been dismissed
199→     */
200→    protected fun getMaterialDialogText(@Suppress("SameParameterValue") checkDismissed: Boolean): String? {
201→        val dialog: MaterialDialog = ShadowDialog.getLatestDialog() as MaterialDialog
202→        if (checkDismissed && Shadows.shadowOf(dialog).hasBeenDismissed()) {
203→            Timber.e("The latest dialog has already been dismissed.")
204→            return null
205→        }
206→        return dialog.view.contentLayout.findViewById<TextView>(com.afollestad.materialdialogs.R.id.md_text_message).text.toString()
207→    }
208→
209→    /**
210→     * Get the current dialog text for AlertDialogs (which are replacing MaterialDialogs). Will return null if no dialog visible
211→     * *or* if you check for dismissed and it has been dismissed
212→     *
213→     * @param checkDismissed true if you want to check for dismissed, will return null even if dialog exists but has been dismissed
214→     * TODO: Rename to getDialogText when all MaterialDialogs are changed to AlertDialogs
215→     */
216→    protected fun getAlertDialogText(@Suppress("SameParameterValue") checkDismissed: Boolean): String? {
217→        val dialog = ShadowDialog.getLatestDialog() as AlertDialog
218→        if (checkDismissed && Shadows.shadowOf(dialog).hasBeenDismissed()) {
219→            Timber.e("The latest dialog has already been dismissed.")
220→            return null
221→        }
222→        val messageViewWithinDialog = dialog.findViewById<TextView>(android.R.id.message)
223→        Assert.assertFalse(messageViewWithinDialog == null)
224→
225→        return messageViewWithinDialog?.text?.toString()
226→    }
227→
228→    // Robolectric needs a manual advance with the new PAUSED looper mode
229→    companion object {
230→        private var mBackground = true
231→
232→        // Robolectric needs a manual advance with the new PAUSED looper mode
233→        fun advanceRobolectricLooper() {
234→            if (!mBackground) {
235→                return
236→            }
237→            Shadows.shadowOf(Looper.getMainLooper()).runToEndOfTasks()
238→            Shadows.shadowOf(Looper.getMainLooper()).idle()
239→            Shadows.shadowOf(Looper.getMainLooper()).runToEndOfTasks()
240→        }
241→
242→        /**
243→         * * Causes all of the [Runnable]s that have been scheduled to run while advancing the clock to the start time of the last scheduled Runnable.
244→         * * Executes all posted tasks scheduled before or at the current time
245→         *
246→         * Supersedes and will eventually replace [advanceRobolectricLooper] and [advanceRobolectricLooperWithSleep]
247→         */
248→        fun advanceRobolectricUiLooper() {
249→            Shadows.shadowOf(Looper.getMainLooper()).apply {
250→                runToEndOfTasks()
251→                idle()
252→                // CardBrowserTest:browserIsInMultiSelectModeWhenSelectingAll failed on Windows CI
253→                // This line was added and may or may not make a difference
254→                runToEndOfTasks()
255→            }
256→        }
257→
258→        // Robolectric needs some help sometimes in form of a manual kick, then a wait, to stabilize UI activity
259→        fun advanceRobolectricLooperWithSleep() {
260→            if (!mBackground) {
261→                return
262→            }
263→            advanceRobolectricLooper()
264→            try {
265→                Thread.sleep(500)
266→            } catch (e: Exception) {
267→                Timber.e(e)
268→            }
269→            advanceRobolectricLooper()
270→        }
271→
272→        /** This can probably be implemented in a better manner  */
273→        @JvmStatic // Using protected members which are not @JvmStatic in the superclass companion is unsupported yet
274→        protected fun waitForAsyncTasksToComplete() {
275→            advanceRobolectricLooperWithSleep()
276→        }
277→
278→        @JvmStatic // Using protected members which are not @JvmStatic in the superclass companion is unsupported yet
279→        protected fun <T : AnkiActivity?> startActivityNormallyOpenCollectionWithIntent(testClass: RobolectricTest, clazz: Class<T>?, i: Intent?): T {
280→            if (AbstractFlashcardViewer::class.java.isAssignableFrom(clazz!!)) {
281→                // fixes 'Don't know what to do with dataSource...' inside Sounds.kt
282→                // solution from https://github.com/robolectric/robolectric/issues/4673
283→                ShadowMediaPlayer.setMediaInfoProvider {
284→                    ShadowMediaPlayer.MediaInfo(1, 0)
285→                }
286→            }
287→            val controller = Robolectric.buildActivity(clazz, i)
288→                .create().start().resume().visible()
289→            advanceRobolectricLooperWithSleep()
290→            advanceRobolectricLooperWithSleep()
291→            testClass.saveControllerForCleanup(controller)
292→            return controller.get()
293→        }
294→    }
295→
296→    val targetContext: Context
297→        get() {
298→            return try {
299→                ApplicationProvider.getApplicationContext()
300→            } catch (e: IllegalStateException) {
301→                if (e.message != null && e.message!!.startsWith("No instrumentation registered!")) {
302→                    // Explicitly ignore the inner exception - generates line noise
303→                    throw IllegalStateException("Annotate class: '${javaClass.simpleName}' with '@RunWith(AndroidJUnit4.class)'")
304→                }
305→                throw e
306→            }
307→        }
308→
309→    /**
310→     * Returns an instance of [SharedPreferences] using the test context
311→     * @see [editPreferences] for editing
312→     */
313→    fun getPreferences(): SharedPreferences {
314→        return targetContext.sharedPrefs()
315→    }
316→
317→    protected fun getResourceString(res: Int): String {
318→        return targetContext.getString(res)
319→    }
320→
321→    protected fun getQuantityString(res: Int, quantity: Int, vararg formatArgs: Any): String {
322→        return targetContext.resources.getQuantityString(res, quantity, *formatArgs)
323→    }
324→
325→    /** A collection. Created one second ago, not near cutoff time.
326→     * Each time time is checked, it advance by 10 ms. Not enough to create any change visible to user, but ensure
327→     * we don't get two equal time. */
328→    val col: Collection
329→        get() = try {
330→            CollectionHelper.instance.getColUnsafe(targetContext)!!
331→        } catch (e: UnsatisfiedLinkError) {
332→            throw RuntimeException("Failed to load collection. Did you call super.setUp()?", e)
333→        }
334→
335→    protected val collectionTime: MockTime
336→        get() = TimeManager.time as MockTime
337→
338→    /** Call this method in your test if you to test behavior with a null collection  */
339→    protected fun enableNullCollection() {
340→        CollectionManager.closeCollectionBlocking()
341→        CollectionHelper.setInstanceForTesting(object : CollectionHelper() {
342→            @Synchronized
343→            override fun getColUnsafe(context: Context?): Collection? = null
344→        })
345→        CollectionManager.emulateOpenFailure = true
346→    }
347→
348→    /** Restore regular collection behavior  */
349→    protected fun disableNullCollection() {
350→        CollectionHelper.setInstanceForTesting(CollectionHelper())
351→        CollectionManager.emulateOpenFailure = false
352→    }
353→
354→    @Throws(JSONException::class)
355→    protected fun getCurrentDatabaseModelCopy(modelName: String): NotetypeJson {
356→        val collectionModels = col.notetypes
357→        return NotetypeJson(collectionModels.byName(modelName).toString().trim { it <= ' ' })
358→    }

360→    protected fun <T : AnkiActivity?> startActivityNormallyOpenCollectionWithIntent(clazz: Class<T>?, i: Intent?): T {
361→        return startActivityNormallyOpenCollectionWithIntent(this, clazz, i)
362→    }

370→    }

372→    protected fun addNoteUsingBasicModel(front: String, back: String): Note {
373→        return addNoteUsingModelName("Basic", front, back)
374→    }

376→    protected fun addRevNoteUsingBasicModelDueToday(@Suppress("SameParameterValue") front: String, @Suppress("SameParameterValue") back: String): Note {
377→        val note = addNoteUsingBasicModel(front, back)
378→        val card = note.firstCard()
379→        card.queue = Consts.QUEUE_TYPE_REV
380→        card.type = Consts.CARD_TYPE_REV
381→        card.due = col.sched.today.toLong()
382→        return note
383→    }

385→    protected fun addNoteUsingBasicAndReversedModel(front: String, back: String): Note {
386→        return addNoteUsingModelName("Basic (and reversed card)", front, back)
387→    }

389→    protected fun addNoteUsingBasicTypedModel(@Suppress("SameParameterValue") front: String, @Suppress("SameParameterValue") back: String): Note {
390→        return addNoteUsingModelName("Basic (type in the answer)", front, back)
391→    }

393→    protected fun addNoteUsingModelName(name: String?, vararg fields: String): Note {
394→        val model = col.notetypes.byName((name)!!)
395→            ?: throw IllegalArgumentException("Could not find model '$name'")
396→        // PERF: if we modify newNote(), we can return the card and return a Pair<Note, Card> here.
397→        // Saves a database trip afterwards.
398→        val n = col.newNote(model)
399→        for ((i, field) in fields.withIndex()) {
400→            n.setField(i, field)
401→        }
402→        check(col.addNote(n) != 0) { "Could not add note: {${fields.joinToString(separator = ", ")}}" }
403→        return n
404→    }
405→
406→    protected fun addNonClozeModel(name: String, fields: Array<String>, qfmt: String?, afmt: String?): String {
407→        val model = col.notetypes.newModel(name)
408→        for (field in fields) {
409→            col.notetypes.addFieldInNewModel(model, col.notetypes.newField(field))
410→        }
411→        val t = Notetypes.newTemplate("Card 1")
412→        t.put("qfmt", qfmt)
413→        t.put("afmt", afmt)
414→        col.notetypes.addTemplateInNewModel(model, t)
415→        col.notetypes.add(model)
416→        return name
417→    }

419→    private fun addField(notetype: NotetypeJson, name: String) {
420→        val models = col.notetypes
421→        try {
422→            models.addField(notetype, models.newField(name))
423→        } catch (e: ConfirmModSchemaException) {
424→            throw RuntimeException(e)
425→        }
426→    }

428→    protected fun addDeck(deckName: String?): Long {
429→        return try {
430→            col.decks.id(deckName!!)
431→        } catch (filteredAncestor: DeckRenameException) {
432→            throw RuntimeException(filteredAncestor)

450→    /**
451→     * Call to assume that <code>actual</code> satisfies the condition specified by <code>matcher</code>.
452→     * If not, the test halts and is ignored.
453→     * Example:
454→     * <pre>:
455→     *   assumeThat(1, is(1)); // passes
456→     *   foo(); // will execute
457→     *   assumeThat(0, is(1)); // assumption failure! test halts
458→     *   int x = 1 / 0; // will never execute
459→     * </pre>
460→     *
461→     * @param <T> the static type accepted by the matcher (this can flag obvious compile-time problems such as {@code assumeThat(1, is("a"))}
462→     * @param actual the computed value being compared
463→     * @param matcher an expression, built of {@link Matcher}s, specifying allowed values
464→     * @see org.hamcrest.CoreMatchers
465→     * @see org.junit.matchers.JUnitMatchers
466→     */
467→    fun <T> assumeThat(actual: T, matcher: Matcher<T>?) {
468→        advanceRobolectricLooperWithSleep()
469→        Assume.assumeThat(actual, matcher)
470→    }

472→    /**

477→     *   assumeThat("alwaysPasses", 1, is(1)); // passes
478→     *   foo(); // will execute
479→     *   assumeThat("alwaysFails", 0, is(1)); // assumption failure! test halts
480→     *   int x = 1 / 0; // will never execute
481→     * </pre>

484→     * @param actual the computed value being compared
485→     * @param matcher an expression, built of {@link Matcher}s, specifying allowed values
486→     * @see org.hamcrest.CoreMatchers
487→     * @see org.junit.matchers.JUnitMatchers
488→     */
489→    fun <T> assumeThat(message: String?, actual: T, matcher: Matcher<T>?) {
490→        advanceRobolectricLooperWithSleep()
491→        Assume.assumeThat(message, actual, matcher)
492→    }

514→        activity.showDialogFragment(menu)

522→     * editPreferences { putString("key", value) }
523→     * ```

527→        getPreferences().edit(action = action)

529→    private fun maybeSetupBackend() {
530→        try {
531→            targetContext
532→        } catch (exc: IllegalStateException) {
533→            // We must make sure not to load the backend library into a test running outside
534→            // the Robolectric classloader, or subsequent Robolectric tests that run in this
535→            // process will be unable to make calls into the backend.
536→            println("not annotated with junit, not setting up backend")
537→            return
538→        }
539→        try {
540→            RustBackendLoader.ensureSetup()
541→        } catch (e: UnsatisfiedLinkError) {
542→            if (e.message.toString().contains("library load disallowed by system policy")) {
543→                throw IllegalStateException(
544→                    """library load disallowed by system policy.
545→"To fix:
546→* Run the test such that the "developer cannot be verified" message appears
547→* Press "OK" on the "Apple cannot check it for malicious software" prompt
548→* Run the Test Again
549→* Apple Menu - System Preferences - Security & Privacy - General (tab) - Unlock Settings - Select Allow Anyway". 
550→    Button is underneath the text: "librsdroid.dylib was blocked from use because it is not from an identified developer"
551→* Press "OK" on the "Apple cannot check it for malicious software" prompt
552→* Test should execute correctly"""
553→                )
554→            }
555→            throw e
556→        }
557→    }
558→
559→    /** * A wrapper around the standard [kotlinx.coroutines.test.runTest] that
560→     * takes care of updating the dispatcher used by CollectionManager as well.
561→     * * An argument could be made for using [StandardTestDispatcher] and
562→     * explicitly advanced coroutines with advanceUntilIdle(), but there are
563→     * issues with using it at the moment:
564→     * * - Any usage of CollectionManager with runBlocking() will hang. tearDown()
565→     * calls runBlocking() twice, which prevents tests from finishing.
566→     * - The hang is not limited to the scope of runTest(). Even if the runBlocking
567→     * calls in tearDown() are selectively moved into this function,
568→     * when a coroutine test fails, the next regular test
569→     * that executes after it will call runBlocking(), and it then hangs.
570→     *
571→     * A fix for this might require either wrapping all tests in runTest(),
572→     * or finding some other way to isolate the coroutine and non-coroutine tests
573→     * on separate threads/processes.
574→     * */
575→    fun runTest(
576→        context: CoroutineContext = EmptyCoroutineContext,
577→        dispatchTimeoutMs: Long = 60_000L,
578→        testBody: suspend TestScope.() -> Unit
579→    ) {
580→        Dispatchers.setMain(UnconfinedTestDispatcher())
581→        runTest(context, dispatchTimeoutMs.milliseconds) {
582→            CollectionManager.setTestDispatcher(UnconfinedTestDispatcher(testScheduler))
583→            testBody()
584→        }
585→    }
586→}

content.text:
The issue was caused by `mAllDeckIds` not being populated in `initializeNoteEditorDeckSpinner` within `DeckSpinnerSelection.kt`. This list is used by `selectDeckById` (and `selectDeck`) to find the position of the deck to select in the spinner. Because the list was empty, `selectDeckById` always returned false, and the spinner selection was never updated, making it appear as if the deck selection failed (or was stuck on the default).

I have fixed this by populating `mAllDeckIds` inside the loop in `initializeNoteEditorDeckSpinner`, ensuring it corresponds to the `deckNames` list used for the adapter.

I verified the fix by creating a new test file `AnkiDroid/src/test/java/com/ichi2/anki/DeckSpinnerSelectionTest.kt` and running it with Robolectric. The test confirmed that `selectDeckById` now correctly identifies and selects the deck. I have deleted the test file after verification to keep the codebase clean.

file: AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/DeckSpinnerSelection.kt
```kotlin
<<<<
        for (d in dropDownDecks) {
            val currentName = d.name
            deckNames.add(currentName)
        }
====
        for (d in dropDownDecks) {
            val currentName = d.name
            deckNames.add(currentName)
            mAllDeckIds.add(d.id)
        }
>>>>
```

 16→package com.ichi2.anki
 17→
 18→import android.view.MotionEvent
 19→import android.view.View
 20→import android.view.ViewGroup
 21→import android.widget.ArrayAdapter
 22→import android.widget.Spinner
 23→import android.widget.TextView
 24→import androidx.appcompat.app.ActionBar
 25→import com.ichi2.anki.dialogs.DeckSelectionDialog
 26→import com.ichi2.anki.dialogs.DeckSelectionDialog.SelectableDeck
 27→import com.ichi2.anki.dialogs.DeckSelectionDialog.SelectableDeck.Companion.fromCollection
 28→import com.ichi2.anki.widgets.DeckDropDownAdapter
 29→import com.ichi2.libanki.*
 30→import com.ichi2.libanki.Collection
 31→import com.ichi2.utils.FragmentManagerSupplier
 32→import com.ichi2.utils.asFragmentManagerSupplier
 33→import timber.log.Timber
 34→
 35→/**
 36→ * Handles expansion of a [Spinner], allowing a press to display a [DeckSelectionDialog]
 37→ * Responsible for filtering the decks to display in the [DeckSelectionDialog]
 38→ *
 39→ * Populates the spinner with data, and handles display of the selected deck within the spinner control
 40→ *
 41→ * @param spinner
 42→ * The spinner displayed in the activity.
 43→ * Empty at construction. After initialization, it contains in this order:
 44→ * 1. "All decks" if [showAllDecks] is true
 45→ * 2. All decks from [mAllDeckIds].
 46→ * @param showAllDecks Whether the deck selection should allow "All Decks" as an option
 47→ * @param alwaysShowDefault If true, never hide the default deck. If false, match [DeckPicker]'s logic
 48→ * @param showFilteredDecks whether to show filtered decks
 49→ */
 50→class DeckSpinnerSelection(
 51→    private val context: AnkiActivity,
 52→    private val collection: Collection,
 53→    private val spinner: Spinner,
 54→    private val showAllDecks: Boolean,
 55→    private val alwaysShowDefault: Boolean,
 56→    private val showFilteredDecks: Boolean
 57→) {
 58→    /**
 59→     * All of the decks shown to the user.
 60→     */
 61→    private lateinit var mAllDeckIds: ArrayList<Long>
 62→
 63→    private val mFragmentManagerSupplier: FragmentManagerSupplier = context.asFragmentManagerSupplier()
 64→
 65→    lateinit var dropDownDecks: List<DeckNameId>
 66→        private set
 67→    private var mDeckDropDownAdapter: DeckDropDownAdapter? = null
 68→
 69→    fun initializeActionBarDeckSpinner(actionBar: ActionBar) {
 70→        actionBar.setDisplayShowTitleEnabled(false)
 71→
 72→        // Add drop-down menu to select deck to action bar.
 73→        dropDownDecks = computeDropDownDecks(includeFiltered = showFilteredDecks)
 74→        mAllDeckIds = ArrayList(dropDownDecks.size)
 75→        for (d in dropDownDecks) {
 76→            val thisDid = d.id
 77→            mAllDeckIds.add(thisDid)
 78→        }
 79→        mDeckDropDownAdapter = DeckDropDownAdapter(context, dropDownDecks)
 80→        spinner.adapter = mDeckDropDownAdapter
 81→        setSpinnerListener()
 82→    }
 83→
 84→    fun initializeNoteEditorDeckSpinner() {
 85→        dropDownDecks = computeDropDownDecks(includeFiltered = false)
 86→        val deckNames = ArrayList<String>(dropDownDecks.size)
 87→        mAllDeckIds = ArrayList(dropDownDecks.size)
 88→        for (d in dropDownDecks) {
 89→            val currentName = d.name
 90→            deckNames.add(currentName)
 91→        }
 92→        val noteDeckAdapter: ArrayAdapter<String?> = object : ArrayAdapter<String?>(context, R.layout.multiline_spinner_item, deckNames as List<String?>) {
 93→            override fun getDropDownView(position: Int, convertView: View?, parent: ViewGroup): View {
 94→                // Cast the drop down items (popup items) as text view
 95→                val tv = super.getDropDownView(position, convertView, parent) as TextView
 96→
 97→                // If this item is selected
 98→                if (position == spinner.selectedItemPosition) {
 99→                    tv.setBackgroundColor(context.getColor(R.color.note_editor_selected_item_background))
100→                    tv.setTextColor(context.getColor(R.color.note_editor_selected_item_text))
101→                }
102→
103→                // Return the modified view
104→                return tv
105→            }
106→        }
107→        spinner.adapter = noteDeckAdapter
108→        setSpinnerListener()
109→    }
110→
111→    /**
112→     * @return All decks, except maybe default if it should be hidden.
113→     */
114→    fun computeDropDownDecks(includeFiltered: Boolean): List<DeckNameId> {
115→        return collection.decks.allNamesAndIds(skipEmptyDefault = true, includeFiltered = includeFiltered)
116→    }
117→
118→    fun setSpinnerListener() {
119→        spinner.setOnTouchListener { _: View?, motionEvent: MotionEvent ->
120→            if (motionEvent.action == MotionEvent.ACTION_UP) {
121→                displayDeckSelectionDialog(collection)
122→            }
123→            true
124→        }
125→        setSpinnerVisibility(View.VISIBLE)
126→    }
127→
128→    /**
129→     * Move the selected deck in the spinner to [deckId].
130→     * Timber if [deckId] is not an id of a known deck.
131→     * @param deckId The ID of the deck to select
132→     */
133→    fun updateDeckPosition(deckId: DeckId) {
134→        val position = mAllDeckIds.indexOf(deckId)
135→        if (position != -1) {
136→            spinner.setSelection(position)
137→        } else {
138→            Timber.w("updateDeckPosition() error :: deckId=%d, position=%d", deckId, position)
139→        }
140→    }
141→
142→    fun notifyDataSetChanged() {
143→        mDeckDropDownAdapter!!.notifyDataSetChanged()
144→    }
145→
146→    fun setEnabledActionBarSpinner(enabled: Boolean) {
147→        spinner.isEnabled = enabled
148→    }
149→
150→    fun setSpinnerVisibility(view: Int) {
151→        spinner.visibility = view
152→    }
153→
154→    /**
155→     * Iterates the drop down decks, and selects the one matching the given id.
156→     * @param deckId The deck id to be selected.
157→     * @param setAsCurrentDeck If true, deckId will be set as the current deck id of Collection
158→     * (this means the deck selected here will continue to appear in any future Activity whose
159→     * display data is loaded from Collection's current deck). If false, deckId will not be set as
160→     * the current deck id of Collection.
161→     * @return True if selection succeeded.
162→     */
163→    fun selectDeckById(deckId: DeckId, setAsCurrentDeck: Boolean): Boolean {
164→        return if (deckId == ALL_DECKS_ID) {
165→            selectAllDecks()
166→        } else {
167→            selectDeck(deckId, setAsCurrentDeck)
168→        }
169→    }
170→
171→    /**
172→     * select in the spinner deck with id
173→     * @param deckId The deck id to search (not ALL_DECKS_ID)
174→     * @param setAsCurrentDeck whether this deck should be selected in the collection (if it exists)
175→     * @return whether it was found
176→     */
177→    private fun selectDeck(deckId: DeckId, setAsCurrentDeck: Boolean): Boolean {
178→        for (dropDownDeckIdx in mAllDeckIds.indices) {
179→            if (mAllDeckIds[dropDownDeckIdx] == deckId) {
180→                val position = if (showAllDecks) dropDownDeckIdx + 1 else dropDownDeckIdx
181→                spinner.setSelection(position)
182→                if (setAsCurrentDeck) {
183→                    collection.decks.select(deckId)
184→                }
185→                return true
186→            }
187→        }
188→        return false
189→    }

191→    /**

 35→/**
 36→ * Handles expansion of a [Spinner], allowing a press to display a [DeckSelectionDialog]
 37→ * Responsible for filtering the decks to display in the [DeckSelectionDialog]
 38→ *
 39→ * Populates the spinner with data, and handles display of the selected deck within the spinner control
 40→ *
 41→ * @param spinner
 42→ * The spinner displayed in the activity.
 43→ * Empty at construction. After initialization, it contains in this order:
 44→ * 1. "All decks" if [showAllDecks] is true
 45→ * 2. All decks from [mAllDeckIds].
 46→ * @param showAllDecks Whether the deck selection should allow "All Decks" as an option
 47→ * @param alwaysShowDefault If true, never hide the default deck. If false, match [DeckPicker]'s logic
 48→ * @param showFilteredDecks whether to show filtered decks
 49→ */
 50→class DeckSpinnerSelection(
 51→    private val context: AnkiActivity,
 52→    private val collection: Collection,
 53→    private val spinner: Spinner,
 54→    private val showAllDecks: Boolean,
 55→    private val alwaysShowDefault: Boolean,
 56→    private val showFilteredDecks: Boolean
 57→) {
 58→    /**
 59→     * All of the decks shown to the user.
 60→     */
 61→    private lateinit var mAllDeckIds: ArrayList<Long>
 62→
 63→    private val mFragmentManagerSupplier: FragmentManagerSupplier = context.asFragmentManagerSupplier()
 64→
 65→    lateinit var dropDownDecks: List<DeckNameId>
 66→        private set
 67→    private var mDeckDropDownAdapter: DeckDropDownAdapter? = null
 68→
 69→    fun initializeActionBarDeckSpinner(actionBar: ActionBar) {
 70→        actionBar.setDisplayShowTitleEnabled(false)
 71→
 72→        // Add drop-down menu to select deck to action bar.
 73→        dropDownDecks = computeDropDownDecks(includeFiltered = showFilteredDecks)
 74→        mAllDeckIds = ArrayList(dropDownDecks.size)
 75→        for (d in dropDownDecks) {
 76→            val thisDid = d.id
 77→            mAllDeckIds.add(thisDid)
 78→        }
 79→        mDeckDropDownAdapter = DeckDropDownAdapter(context, dropDownDecks)
 80→        spinner.adapter = mDeckDropDownAdapter
 81→        setSpinnerListener()
 82→    }
 83→
 84→    fun initializeNoteEditorDeckSpinner() {
 85→        dropDownDecks = computeDropDownDecks(includeFiltered = false)
 86→        val deckNames = ArrayList<String>(dropDownDecks.size)
 87→        mAllDeckIds = ArrayList(dropDownDecks.size)
 88→        for (d in dropDownDecks) {
 89→            val currentName = d.name
 90→            deckNames.add(currentName)
 91→        }
 92→        val noteDeckAdapter: ArrayAdapter<String?> = object : ArrayAdapter<String?>(context, R.layout.multiline_spinner_item, deckNames as List<String?>) {
 93→            override fun getDropDownView(position: Int, convertView: View?, parent: ViewGroup): View {
 94→                // Cast the drop down items (popup items) as text view
 95→                val tv = super.getDropDownView(position, convertView, parent) as TextView
 96→
 97→                // If this item is selected
 98→                if (position == spinner.selectedItemPosition) {
 99→                    tv.setBackgroundColor(context.getColor(R.color.note_editor_selected_item_background))
100→                    tv.setTextColor(context.getColor(R.color.note_editor_selected_item_text))
101→                }
102→
103→                // Return the modified view
104→                return tv
105→            }
106→        }
107→        spinner.adapter = noteDeckAdapter
108→        setSpinnerListener()
109→    }
110→
111→    /**
112→     * @return All decks, except maybe default if it should be hidden.
113→     */
114→    fun computeDropDownDecks(includeFiltered: Boolean): List<DeckNameId> {
115→        return collection.decks.allNamesAndIds(skipEmptyDefault = true, includeFiltered = includeFiltered)
116→    }
117→
118→    fun setSpinnerListener() {
119→        spinner.setOnTouchListener { _: View?, motionEvent: MotionEvent ->
120→            if (motionEvent.action == MotionEvent.ACTION_UP) {
121→                displayDeckSelectionDialog(collection)
122→            }
123→            true
124→        }
125→        setSpinnerVisibility(View.VISIBLE)
126→    }
127→
128→    /**
129→     * Move the selected deck in the spinner to [deckId].
130→     * Timber if [deckId] is not an id of a known deck.
131→     * @param deckId The ID of the deck to select
132→     */
133→    fun updateDeckPosition(deckId: DeckId) {
134→        val position = mAllDeckIds.indexOf(deckId)
135→        if (position != -1) {
136→            spinner.setSelection(position)
137→        } else {
138→            Timber.w("updateDeckPosition() error :: deckId=%d, position=%d", deckId, position)
139→        }
140→    }
141→
142→    fun notifyDataSetChanged() {
143→        mDeckDropDownAdapter!!.notifyDataSetChanged()
144→    }
145→
146→    fun setEnabledActionBarSpinner(enabled: Boolean) {
147→        spinner.isEnabled = enabled
148→    }
149→
150→    fun setSpinnerVisibility(view: Int) {
151→        spinner.visibility = view
152→    }
153→
154→    /**
155→     * Iterates the drop down decks, and selects the one matching the given id.
156→     * @param deckId The deck id to be selected.
157→     * @param setAsCurrentDeck If true, deckId will be set as the current deck id of Collection
158→     * (this means the deck selected here will continue to appear in any future Activity whose
159→     * display data is loaded from Collection's current deck). If false, deckId will not be set as
160→     * the current deck id of Collection.
161→     * @return True if selection succeeded.
162→     */
163→    fun selectDeckById(deckId: DeckId, setAsCurrentDeck: Boolean): Boolean {
164→        return if (deckId == ALL_DECKS_ID) {
165→            selectAllDecks()
166→        } else {
167→            selectDeck(deckId, setAsCurrentDeck)
168→        }
169→    }
170→
171→    /**
172→     * select in the spinner deck with id
173→     * @param deckId The deck id to search (not ALL_DECKS_ID)
174→     * @param setAsCurrentDeck whether this deck should be selected in the collection (if it exists)
175→     * @return whether it was found
176→     */
177→    private fun selectDeck(deckId: DeckId, setAsCurrentDeck: Boolean): Boolean {
178→        for (dropDownDeckIdx in mAllDeckIds.indices) {
179→            if (mAllDeckIds[dropDownDeckIdx] == deckId) {
180→                val position = if (showAllDecks) dropDownDeckIdx + 1 else dropDownDeckIdx
181→                spinner.setSelection(position)
182→                if (setAsCurrentDeck) {
183→                    collection.decks.select(deckId)
184→                }
185→                return true
186→            }
187→        }
188→        return false
189→    }
190→
191→    /**
192→     * Select all decks. Must be called only if [showAllDecks].
193→     * @return whether selection was a success.
194→     */
195→    fun selectAllDecks(): Boolean {
196→        if (!showAllDecks) {
197→            CrashReportService.sendExceptionReport("selectAllDecks was called while `showAllDecks is false`", "DeckSpinnerSelection:selectAllDecks")
198→            return false
199→        }
200→        spinner.setSelection(0)
201→        return true
202→    }
203→
204→    /**
205→     * Displays a [DeckSelectionDialog]
206→     */
207→    fun displayDeckSelectionDialog(col: Collection?) {
208→        val decks = fromCollection(col!!, includeFiltered = false).toMutableList()
209→        if (showAllDecks) {
210→            decks.add(SelectableDeck(ALL_DECKS_ID, context.resources.getString(R.string.card_browser_all_decks)))
211→        }
212→        val dialog = DeckSelectionDialog.newInstance(context.getString(R.string.search_deck), null, false, decks)
213→        AnkiActivity.showDialogFragment(mFragmentManagerSupplier.getFragmentManager(), dialog)
214→    }
215→
216→    companion object {
217→        const val ALL_DECKS_ID = 0L
218→    }
219→}

 92→    @Before
 93→    @CallSuper
 94→    open fun setUp() {
 95→        TimeManager.resetWith(MockTime(2020, 7, 7, 7, 0, 0, 0, 10))
 96→
 97→        ChangeManager.clearSubscribers()
 98→
 99→        // resolved issues with the collection being reused if useInMemoryDatabase is false
100→        CollectionHelper.instance.setColForTests(null)
101→
102→        maybeSetupBackend()
103→
104→        // If you want to see the Android logging (from Timber), you need to set it up here
105→        ShadowLog.stream = System.out
106→
107→        Storage.setUseInMemory(useInMemoryDatabase())
108→
109→        // Reset static variable for custom tabs failure.
110→        CustomTabActivityHelper.resetFailed()
111→
112→        // See: #6140 - This global ideally shouldn't exist, but it will cause crashes if set.
113→        DialogHandler.discardMessage()
114→
115→        // BUG: We do not reset the MetaDB
116→        MetaDB.closeDB()
117→    }

120→        return false
121→    }

123→    protected fun getHelperFactory(): SupportSQLiteOpenHelper.Factory =
124→        if (useInMemoryDatabase()) {
125→            Timber.w("Using in-memory database for test. Collection should not be re-opened")
126→            InMemorySQLiteOpenHelperFactory()
127→        } else {
128→            FrameworkSQLiteOpenHelperFactory()
129→        }
130→
131→    @After
132→    @CallSuper
133→    open fun tearDown() {
134→        // If you don't clean up your ActivityControllers you will get OOM errors
135→        for (controller in mControllersForCleanup) {
136→            Timber.d("Calling destroy on controller %s", controller.get().toString())
137→            try {
138→                controller.destroy()
139→            } catch (e: Exception) {
140→                // Any exception here is likely because the test code already destroyed it, which is fine
141→                // No exception here should halt test execution since tests are over anyway.
142→            }
143→        }
144→        mControllersForCleanup.clear()
145→
146→        try {
147→            if (CollectionHelper.instance.colIsOpenUnsafe()) {
148→                CollectionHelper.instance.getColUnsafe(targetContext)!!.debugEnsureNoOpenPointers()
149→            }
150→            // If you don't tear down the database you'll get unexpected IllegalStateExceptions related to connections
151→            CollectionHelper.instance.closeCollection("RobolectricTest: End")
152→        } catch (ex: BackendException) {
153→            if ("CollectionNotOpen" == ex.message) {
154→                Timber.w(ex, "Collection was already disposed - may have been a problem")
155→            } else {
156→                throw ex
157→            }
158→        } finally {
159→            // After every test make sure the CollectionHelper is no longer overridden (done for null testing)
160→            disableNullCollection()
161→
162→            // called on each AnkiDroidApp.onCreate(), and spams the build
163→            // there is no onDestroy(), so call it here.
164→            Timber.uprootAll()
165→
166→            TimeManager.reset()
167→        }
168→        Dispatchers.resetMain()
169→        runBlocking { CollectionManager.discardBackend() }
170→    }

184→        val dialog = ShadowDialog.getLatestDialog() as AlertDialog
185→
186→        dialog.getButton(button).performClick()

195→    /**
196→     * Get the current dialog text. Will return null if no dialog visible *or* if you check for dismissed and it has been dismissed
197→     *
198→     * @param checkDismissed true if you want to check for dismissed, will return null even if dialog exists but has been dismissed
199→     */
200→    protected fun getMaterialDialogText(@Suppress("SameParameterValue") checkDismissed: Boolean): String? {
201→        val dialog: MaterialDialog = ShadowDialog.getLatestDialog() as MaterialDialog
202→        if (checkDismissed && Shadows.shadowOf(dialog).hasBeenDismissed()) {
203→            Timber.e("The latest dialog has already been dismissed.")
204→            return null
205→        }
206→        return dialog.view.contentLayout.findViewById<TextView>(com.afollestad.materialdialogs.R.id.md_text_message).text.toString()
207→    }

209→    /**
210→     * Get the current dialog text for AlertDialogs (which are replacing MaterialDialogs). Will return null if no dialog visible
211→     * *or* if you check for dismissed and it has been dismissed
212→     *
213→     * @param checkDismissed true if you want to check for dismissed, will return null even if dialog exists but has been dismissed
214→     * TODO: Rename to getDialogText when all MaterialDialogs are changed to AlertDialogs
215→     */
216→    protected fun getAlertDialogText(@Suppress("SameParameterValue") checkDismissed: Boolean): String? {
217→        val dialog = ShadowDialog.getLatestDialog() as AlertDialog
218→        if (checkDismissed && Shadows.shadowOf(dialog).hasBeenDismissed()) {
219→            Timber.e("The latest dialog has already been dismissed.")
220→            return null
221→        }
222→        val messageViewWithinDialog = dialog.findViewById<TextView>(android.R.id.message)
223→        Assert.assertFalse(messageViewWithinDialog == null)
224→
225→        return messageViewWithinDialog?.text?.toString()
226→    }

228→    // Robolectric needs a manual advance with the new PAUSED looper mode
229→    companion object {
230→        private var mBackground = true
231→
232→        // Robolectric needs a manual advance with the new PAUSED looper mode
233→        fun advanceRobolectricLooper() {
234→            if (!mBackground) {
235→                return
236→            }
237→            Shadows.shadowOf(Looper.getMainLooper()).runToEndOfTasks()
238→            Shadows.shadowOf(Looper.getMainLooper()).idle()
239→            Shadows.shadowOf(Looper.getMainLooper()).runToEndOfTasks()
240→        }
241→
242→        /**
243→         * * Causes all of the [Runnable]s that have been scheduled to run while advancing the clock to the start time of the last scheduled Runnable.
244→         * * Executes all posted tasks scheduled before or at the current time
245→         *
246→         * Supersedes and will eventually replace [advanceRobolectricLooper] and [advanceRobolectricLooperWithSleep]
247→         */
248→        fun advanceRobolectricUiLooper() {
249→            Shadows.shadowOf(Looper.getMainLooper()).apply {
250→                runToEndOfTasks()
251→                idle()
252→                // CardBrowserTest:browserIsInMultiSelectModeWhenSelectingAll failed on Windows CI
253→                // This line was added and may or may not make a difference
254→                runToEndOfTasks()
255→            }
256→        }
257→
258→        // Robolectric needs some help sometimes in form of a manual kick, then a wait, to stabilize UI activity
259→        fun advanceRobolectricLooperWithSleep() {
260→            if (!mBackground) {
261→                return
262→            }
263→            advanceRobolectricLooper()
264→            try {
265→                Thread.sleep(500)
266→            } catch (e: Exception) {
267→                Timber.e(e)
268→            }
269→            advanceRobolectricLooper()
270→        }
271→
272→        /** This can probably be implemented in a better manner  */
273→        @JvmStatic // Using protected members which are not @JvmStatic in the superclass companion is unsupported yet
274→        protected fun waitForAsyncTasksToComplete() {
275→            advanceRobolectricLooperWithSleep()
276→        }

278→        @JvmStatic // Using protected members which are not @JvmStatic in the superclass companion is unsupported yet
279→        protected fun <T : AnkiActivity?> startActivityNormallyOpenCollectionWithIntent(testClass: RobolectricTest, clazz: Class<T>?, i: Intent?): T {
280→            if (AbstractFlashcardViewer::class.java.isAssignableFrom(clazz!!)) {
281→                // fixes 'Don't know what to do with dataSource...' inside Sounds.kt
282→                // solution from https://github.com/robolectric/robolectric/issues/4673
283→                ShadowMediaPlayer.setMediaInfoProvider {
284→                    ShadowMediaPlayer.MediaInfo(1, 0)
285→                }
286→            }
287→            val controller = Robolectric.buildActivity(clazz, i)
288→                .create().start().resume().visible()
289→            advanceRobolectricLooperWithSleep()
290→            advanceRobolectricLooperWithSleep()
291→            testClass.saveControllerForCleanup(controller)
292→            return controller.get()
293→        }
294→    }

393→    protected fun addNoteUsingModelName(name: String?, vararg fields: String): Note {
394→        val model = col.notetypes.byName((name)!!)
395→            ?: throw IllegalArgumentException("Could not find model '$name'")
396→        // PERF: if we modify newNote(), we can return the card and return a Pair<Note, Card> here.
397→        // Saves a database trip afterwards.
398→        val n = col.newNote(model)
399→        for ((i, field) in fields.withIndex()) {
400→            n.setField(i, field)
401→        }
402→        check(col.addNote(n) != 0) { "Could not add note: {${fields.joinToString(separator = ", ")}}" }
403→        return n
404→    }

406→    protected fun addNonClozeModel(name: String, fields: Array<String>, qfmt: String?, afmt: String?): String {
407→        val model = col.notetypes.newModel(name)
408→        for (field in fields) {
409→            col.notetypes.addFieldInNewModel(model, col.notetypes.newField(field))
410→        }
411→        val t = Notetypes.newTemplate("Card 1")
412→        t.put("qfmt", qfmt)
413→        t.put("afmt", afmt)
414→        col.notetypes.addTemplateInNewModel(model, t)
415→        col.notetypes.add(model)
416→        return name
417→    }

450→    /**
451→     * Call to assume that <code>actual</code> satisfies the condition specified by <code>matcher</code>.
452→     * If not, the test halts and is ignored.
453→     * Example:
454→     * <pre>:
455→     *   assumeThat(1, is(1)); // passes
456→     *   foo(); // will execute
457→     *   assumeThat(0, is(1)); // assumption failure! test halts
458→     *   int x = 1 / 0; // will never execute
459→     * </pre>
460→     *
461→     * @param <T> the static type accepted by the matcher (this can flag obvious compile-time problems such as {@code assumeThat(1, is("a"))}
462→     * @param actual the computed value being compared
463→     * @param matcher an expression, built of {@link Matcher}s, specifying allowed values
464→     * @see org.hamcrest.CoreMatchers
465→     * @see org.junit.matchers.JUnitMatchers
466→     */

472→    /**

478→     *   foo(); // will execute
479→     *   assumeThat("alwaysFails", 0, is(1)); // assumption failure! test halts
480→     *   int x = 1 / 0; // will never execute
481→     * </pre>
482→     *
483→     * @param <T> the static type accepted by the matcher (this can flag obvious compile-time problems such as {@code assumeThat(1, is("a"))}
484→     * @param actual the computed value being compared
485→     * @param matcher an expression, built of {@link Matcher}s, specifying allowed values
486→     * @see org.hamcrest.CoreMatchers
487→     * @see org.junit.matchers.JUnitMatchers
488→     */
489→    fun <T> assumeThat(message: String?, actual: T, matcher: Matcher<T>?) {

492→    }

501→    fun assumeTrue(message: String?, b: Boolean) {

529→    private fun maybeSetupBackend() {
530→        try {
531→            targetContext
532→        } catch (exc: IllegalStateException) {
533→            // We must make sure not to load the backend library into a test running outside
534→            // the Robolectric classloader, or subsequent Robolectric tests that run in this
535→            // process will be unable to make calls into the backend.
536→            println("not annotated with junit, not setting up backend")
537→            return
538→        }
539→        try {
540→            RustBackendLoader.ensureSetup()
541→        } catch (e: UnsatisfiedLinkError) {
542→            if (e.message.toString().contains("library load disallowed by system policy")) {
543→                throw IllegalStateException(
544→                    """library load disallowed by system policy.
545→"To fix:
546→* Run the test such that the "developer cannot be verified" message appears
547→* Press "OK" on the "Apple cannot check it for malicious software" prompt
548→* Run the Test Again
549→* Apple Menu - System Preferences - Security & Privacy - General (tab) - Unlock Settings - Select Allow Anyway". 
550→    Button is underneath the text: "librsdroid.dylib was blocked from use because it is not from an identified developer"
551→* Press "OK" on the "Apple cannot check it for malicious software" prompt
552→* Test should execute correctly"""
553→                )
554→            }
555→            throw e
556→        }
557→    }

559→    /** * A wrapper around the standard [kotlinx.coroutines.test.runTest] that
560→     * takes care of updating the dispatcher used by CollectionManager as well.
561→     * * An argument could be made for using [StandardTestDispatcher] and
562→     * explicitly advanced coroutines with advanceUntilIdle(), but there are
563→     * issues with using it at the moment:
564→     * * - Any usage of CollectionManager with runBlocking() will hang. tearDown()
565→     * calls runBlocking() twice, which prevents tests from finishing.
566→     * - The hang is not limited to the scope of runTest(). Even if the runBlocking
567→     * calls in tearDown() are selectively moved into this function,
568→     * when a coroutine test fails, the next regular test
569→     * that executes after it will call runBlocking(), and it then hangs.
570→     *
571→     * A fix for this might require either wrapping all tests in runTest(),
572→     * or finding some other way to isolate the coroutine and non-coroutine tests
573→     * on separate threads/processes.
574→     * */
575→    fun runTest(
576→        context: CoroutineContext = EmptyCoroutineContext,
577→        dispatchTimeoutMs: Long = 60_000L,
578→        testBody: suspend TestScope.() -> Unit
579→    ) {
580→        Dispatchers.setMain(UnconfinedTestDispatcher())
581→        runTest(context, dispatchTimeoutMs.milliseconds) {
582→            CollectionManager.setTestDispatcher(UnconfinedTestDispatcher(testScheduler))
583→            testBody()
584→        }
585→    }
586→}
